page: Amazon Web Services m6g.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by custom AWS Graviton2 processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz with the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. It does not feature local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate solid multi-threaded performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the average category. The custom ARM architecture delivers strong memory bandwidth, reaching 946.94 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 129.28 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff; Geekbench single-core scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression are classified as weak. Cryptographic performance is also limited, as evidenced by a weak Passmark CPU encryption score, though the processor supports hardware-accelerated AES.

The m6g.4xlarge offers a highly cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded, scale-out workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. It is well-suited for application servers, static web serving (achieving 18.69 GB/sec in throughput tests), caching fleets like Redis (demonstrating 18.83 million operations per second), and software compilation. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks, heavy cryptographic operations, or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance is poor to weak.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton2 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a 5 Gbps baseline network speed, with no local storage or GPU hardware. The Neoverse-N1 architecture includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, but lacks AVX or AMX extensions. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU performance and high memory read bandwidth of up to 946.94 GB/sec, though single-core execution and cryptographic operations represent performance tradeoffs. The instance is highly cost-effective for multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for application servers, Redis caching, static web serving, and software compilation. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for cost-efficient multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A cost-efficient ARM64 virtual server featuring sixteen dedicated cores and balanced memory for multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton2 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 64 GB of system memory and 5 Gbps network bandwidth
Delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory read bandwidth
Exhibits lower single-core and cryptographic performance tradeoffs
Best suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and software compilation
